LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EMILIO MONTALVO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE WHICH PLEAD SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT CLAIMS FOR WRONGFUL EVICTION. THE DEMURRER TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF NO LONGER RESIDES IN THE UNIT; THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT THIS ACTION IS BROUGHT FOR THE BENEFIT OF THE PUBLIC AND TAKING PROPERTY LEFT BY A TENANT IS NOT AN UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICE. TEN DAYS TO ANSWER THE COMPLAINT. COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER WITH FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND TO ALLEGE PUNITIVE DAMAGES. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Binafar Jaigirdar of Bledsoe Cathcart Diestel Pedersen & Treppa (981.5411) for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT EMILIO MONTALVO'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for plaintiff to plead defendant's dominant motivation in failing to make repairs. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Provide Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Binafar S. Jaigirdar, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Emilio Montalvo

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Emilio Montalvo's motion to compel special interrogatories and request for production of documents is moot as responses have been served. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $340 shall be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
